spec_match.rationale: Flawless and authentic demonstration of sibling-count() within calc() to scale padding, typography size, and weight purely in CSS.

interactive.rationale: Provides highly responsive density selectors that dynamically change the number of nav items and illustrate real-time CSS rescaling.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Frames the density-scaling problem perfectly as a prime candidate for CSS tree-counting features.

edge_cases.rationale: Successfully validates feature support on load and falls back gracefully to static design defaults.

references.rationale: Fails to include the mandatory chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link.

design_system.rationale: Fully integrates with standard styles, margins, and font tokens.

openQuestions.title: Add mandatory chromestatus.com spec reference link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the mandatory reference link to the ChromeStatus entry. Please add a reference section containing a link to `https://chromestatus.com/feature/6225478530367488`.

summary: A beautiful and authentic demonstration of sibling-count() automatically adjusting the density of a navigation bar in pure CSS. The interactive controls are robust, but it misses the mandatory ChromeStatus spec reference link.
